name: Rhyme & Rasam: Poetry That’s Easy to Love - A Discussion Group
description:
Come, enjoy an evening of poetry with us. This is a guided reading with carefully-chosen poems. We won't torture the poems through over-analysis. We will examine them lightly and read them in context. 
You don't even have to love poetry to join. Come for some literary comfort and some surprises. Depth of flavour assured!
Hosted by writer and poet Suchi Govindarajan
Age: 16+

name: The Sarjapur Blues Band - A musical Evening
description:
Join us at Atta Galatta for an intimate and delightfully offbeat evening with The Sarjapur Blues Band, an underground, eclectic rhythm and blues duo known as much for their music as for their irreverent humour and refusal to play by the rules. With roots going back over three decades on Sarjapur Road, the band has built a quiet, word-of-mouth following, performing only their original music in homes, offices, and beloved cultural spaces.
The Sarjapur Blues Band—featuring Shekhar Seshadri on vocals and acoustic guitar, and Vinoo Matthew on electric guitar—will perform on 29th May, from 6 PM onwards. With over 50 years of performance experience between them, the duo brings a rare mix of lived-in musicality, sharp wit, and storytelling to the stage.
Known for their no-frills, no-recordings, no-internet presence, SBB remains refreshingly elusive in a world of constant visibility. Their performances are peppered with humour and candid banter, with songs like the “world-famous-in-Bangalore” Lady Nicotine offering a glimpse into their unique musical world.
Join us for an evening of music and the unmistakable charm of a band that has quietly become a local legend.
Age: All

name: A STORY WALK through INDIRA NAGAR - Folklore , Heritage and Ecology Walk
description: On a Saturday morning , Walk through the lanes of INDIRA NAGAR with Vikram Sridhar discovering the lanes through the Folklore , Heritage and Ecology of the area . The walk will begin and end at ATTA GALATTA . The tales will revolve around historical , mythical and personal narratives woven as we discover the area through the walk .

name: Food In Literature - Book Club
description:
Food is more than sustenance; it is a map of our memories and a bridge to different cultures. This  month, we continue our dialogue on the narratives that connect what we read to what we eat. What does it mean to understand a civilization through its appetite? 
Book of the month: Hooked by Asako Yuzuki. Translated by Polly Barton
When: Saturday, 30th May, 2026 at 11 a.m.
Where: Atta Galatta Bookstore, Indira Nagar
Ages: 18+
Hosted by: Prachi Grover
Registration is mandatory as we have limited spots (link in bio)
The session is free & copies of the book are available at the store. 
Prachi Grover is a culinary consultant and educator and is the founder of “From Words to Wok”; a community for people who love to read and eat!

Age: 18+

name: For Keeps - A Crochet Workshop
description:
Spend a slow afternoon learning the basics of crochet while making something small, personal, and worth holding onto.
In this beginner-friendly workshop, you’ll create a soft, handmade frame for a photograph — turning it into a tactile keepsake. The process is simple and guided, designed for anyone curious about crochet, even with no prior experience.
Bring along a polaroid photo that means something to you, or choose from a set of quirky images we’ll have at the session. You’re also welcome to send us your photos in advance at slipstitch.india@gmail.com so we can have them ready for you.
You’ll leave with a finished piece, made slowly, and something to keep.
---
Details 📍 Atta Galatta, Bangalore 📅 30 May ⏰ 4:00 PM – 6:00 PM 💰 ₹1100 (all materials included) 🧶 Beginner-friendly
Join us for a mindful making session where you create, connect, and unwind — one loop at a time.
Age: 20-50 yrs
All materials provided

name: ONCE UPON A BRUNCH  - Sunday Morning Meal and Culinary Storytelling
description:
Late weekend morning , brunch , stories like the ones from the olden times , meeting new people
What if they all come together ?
Weekend and late morning Brunch is an emotion . On the other hand Stories have always been an emotion too . What's common between the two is the rich heritage of food and stories. What if they come together? .
Listen to these stories of the food as you enjoy your weekend Brunch set at a cozy location that has its own stories . A beautiful day starts with a lovely breakfast . And a morning with breakfast and stories. Every food we eat has a story to share .. Idli , Vada , bread , Paratha etc . Every breakfast comes after a long break and is eager to be eaten . A simple Weekend morning Brunch put together by Atta Galatta
Culinary storytelling by Vikram Sridhar

name: Symbiocene AI - Book Launch
description:
The launch of Symbiocene AI: Algorithms of Nature marks a thoughtful convergence of technology, ecology, and philosophy, set within an intimate yet intellectually vibrant gathering. Hosted by an artist, the event brings together a diverse audience—practitioners, students, and creative minds—setting the stage for a multidisciplinary dialogue that transcends conventional book launches.
 
The book is unique in its proposition that intelligence in AI need not remain confined to computational mimicry of human cognition, but can be reimagined through the self-organizing, adaptive logics of nature. By expanding the very definition of intelligence, it opens pathways to a more symbiotic, sustainable paradigm of artificial intelligence.
 
A brief discussion follows, featuring perspectives from both a seasoned practitioner and a graduate, juxtaposing real-world applicability with emerging academic curiosity. This exchange offers a layered interpretation of the book’s ideas, grounding theory in practice while inviting fresh inquiry.
 
The session transitions into an author-led walkthrough, offering deeper insights into the book’s structure and intent, followed by an interactive Q&A with the audience. The evening concludes with informal conversations over refreshments, fostering continued exchange across disciplines and perspectives

name: The Cinema Of Bimal Roy - An Evening of Films
description:
The man who spoke in pictures.
The man who was the first to bring shades of grey to the black-and-white screen.
The man who has inspired every filmmaker after him.
An auteur of the Hindi Golden Era, who won 7 Filmfare Awards out of just 14 films he made.
Bimal Roy- the director, editor and cinematographer.
Cinema lovers of Bengaluru, this is your perfect entry point into the timeless world of Bimal Roy.
Join us as we explore some of his greatest works—Parineeta, Madhumati, Sujata and Bandini with film in focus- Do Bigha Zamin.
Dive deep into the stories, emotions, and craft that define his cinema.
This will be a 3-hour immersive experience, where we’ll discuss films, revisit memorable scenes, and even celebrate the melodious songs that continue to live on.
Bring along your favourite moments, sing your favourite songs or celebrate your favourite work of Bimal Roy cinema - or simply come and discover them with us.
📅 31st May
🕔 4:00 PM to 7:00 PM
📍 Atta Galatta

Age: 15+

name: Read A Kitaab - Book Club
description:
From manga shelves and masked vigilantes to memoirs, politics, nostalgia, and heartbreak - this month’s Read A Kitaab meet-up is dedicated to the fascinating universe of comic books & graphic novels.
Whether you love the darkness of Batman, the rebellion of V for Vendetta, the brilliance of Maus, or the warmth of Heartstopper — come join fellow readers for an evening of visual storytelling and vibrant conversations. 
Expect:
📖 Discussions on iconic graphic novels
🖍️ Conversations on art, narrative & symbolism
🌍 Comics from across cultures
☕ Books, coffee, and chaotic reader energy
✨ And people who still believe illustrations can change lives
Bring along your favourite comic/graphic novel if you’d like to share it with the group!
📍Atta Galatta, Indira Nagar, Bengaluru
📅 Sunday, May 31st, 2026
⏰ 5 PM – 7 PM
Entry free. Registration mandatory.

name: Koko’s Colours of India - Storytelling for Children
description:
We're thrilled to bring Koko's Colours of India to life at Atta Galatta, Indiranagar!
Come spend a wonderful Saturday morning with Koko the crow as he discovers himself while exploring the vibrant colours of India. This free event is designed especially for little ones between 3 and 7 years, and packed with activities to spark their imagination.
What's in store: 

 Story read aloud 
Meet the author 
Sing along to Koko's crow song 
Make your own storybook

name: Slow Down. Enjoy the Doodle. Paint with Joy - Watercolour Workshop
description:
Join me for a cozy, beginner-friendly watercolor workshop at Atta Galatta where we’ll slow down, play with colors, and paint whimsical birds and flowers in a relaxed, joyful way.
This is not about perfection.
It’s about taking a pause… letting your creativity breathe… and enjoying the simple magic of watercolor. 💛
No prior experience needed — just bring your curiosity and willingness to explore. I’ll guide you step by step through basic watercolor techniques, doodles, expressive lines, and playful compositions in a warm, pressure-free environment.
🌸 What you’ll experience:
Learn beginner-friendly watercolor basics
Create cute whimsical bird illustrations
Explore doodling and expressive details
Relax, unwind, and enjoy mindful creativity
Take home your own handmade artwork
All materials will be provided, so you can simply come, paint, play, and bloom. 🌼
Whether you’re someone who hasn’t painted in years or someone looking for a peaceful creative break — this workshop is for you.
✨ No pressure. No rules. Just watercolor, doodles, and joy. ✨
Age: 12+
All Materials Provided

name: Toy Story - A Zine Workshop
description:
We often get so caught up in routines, responsibilities, and the seriousness of everyday life that we forget the joy of sitting with toys and building whimsical worlds made of imaginary creatures, misadventures and make-believe stories...our personal Disneyland where absolutely anything is possible!

This workshop is an invitation to be silly and curious,  to daydream together, invent impossible worlds through play, storytelling, and zine-making. Bring a toy or two to share with your playmates.

@that_thing_i_do is a comic maker, graphic designer, and curator of curious things who collects toys from wherever she goes and spends hours dreaming up tiny worlds and whimsical stories with them. For this workshop, she will be bringing along her personal collection of toys, characters, and strange little objects to share with you. She loves to make things for her own amusement.
Age: 18+

name: Atta Galatta presents “The BWW Short Story Community” - Literary Meetup
description:
The BWW Short Fiction Community at Atta Galatta will discuss
Stick Season
By Jenny Xie
Quietly observant and emotionally precise, Stick Season follows moments of distance, memory, and fragile human connection against the stark stillness of winter. Through intimate reflections and finely detailed observations, the story explores themes of loneliness, displacement, and the subtle ways people attempt to understand one another while carrying their own private worlds. Jenny Xie’s restrained yet evocative prose captures the ache of transition and the beauty hidden within ordinary moments.
Read the text and come for the event.

name: What I Wish I Had Know - An evening of honest sharing: across ages, across life stages.
description:
The most important things we know weren't learned from books. They came from a decision that backfired, a phase that was hard, or a moment we figured out just barely in time.
Most of us carry wisdom we wish had come sooner. And guess what? Most of us don't realise someone out there is still waiting to hear exactly what we've already lived through.
Not the neat version. The real one, even if it sounds messy.
Come share. Come listen. And remember, you were never the only one who didn't have it all figured out. Your story belongs in this room.

Host Details:
Mamtha Rajesh is the founder of Utsmaya and a psychotherapist with 26 years of experience working intensively with 1000s of clients across 16 countries, cultures, and age groups. 
Mihika Rajesh is a Psychotherapist working with individuals supporting them in understanding their inner worlds and is trained in inner child healing. She holds an MSC in Mental Health from the University of Edinburgh.

Age: 18+

name: Indomitable - Book Launch
description:
Atta Galatta invites you to the 1
st book reading session of Indomitable by Yezdi Billimoria - a 
reading of select chapters, coffee and conversations that will make this a memorable evening. 
Readers who love stories by Mitch Albom, Fredrick Backman, Eli Shafak may find similar 
threads of emotional resonance within the pages of this book.
Indomitable is the story of Jamshed Irani - a boy with a mercurial temper and sky-high 
ambition to fly unfiltered among blue skies. Along the way to his goal, a hot-headed, young 
Jamshed falls in love with the calm and innocent Zenobia. Together they form the core of the 
story as they come together in love and are ultimately separated by the cold reality of death. 
At its core, Indomitable is a hearƞelt story of middle-class aspirations and the travails of 
everyday living - of family, love & laughter coupled with loneliness & death. 
About the author 
Yezdi is a bookworm, a music buff, and an emerging author with an insatiable desire for books. 
His love for the written word spans diverse authors and genres - from the rivting thrillers of 
James Hadley Chase & John Grisham to the evil, yet wickedly satisfying tales of Stephen King, 
and from the rollicking happenings of James Herriot to the uplifting prose of Neale Donald 
Walsche, he is always on the lookout for the next great novel to sink his teeth into. 
Apart from books, he is a music buff and loves the Beatles & Dire Straits as much as he does 
the timeless melodies of Kishore and Rafi.
All in all, Yezdi is a full time bookworm, part time author, a foodie and a dreamer. To know 
more about his writing, check out his Website & Instagram @ yezdisworld. 
Age: 30+

name: Climate Anxiety & Art - Arts Based Reflective Workshop
description:
What is climate anxiety? What does it feel like in your body? Most importantly, what can we do about this overwhelming feeling?

Bangalore’s changing landscape has brought up collective helplessness, anger, guilt and fear. If you also feel the problem is larger than your capacity to solve it, I’ve found some answers in feminist psychology. As a mental health professional, I am offering tools for us to sit with ourselves in the middle of a burning world.

🗓️: 6th June (Saturday)
⏰: 6:30pm to 7:30pm
📍: Atta Galatta, Indiranagar

Join us for an arts based reflective workshop on climate anxiety to work through our feelings.

Age:18+

name: Lahe Lahe Caravan presents “Harry Potter Book Discussion” - Meetup
description:
For hard-core Potterheads age 12 and above!
Get into the minds of the characters, ferret out the intricacies of the plot - enter the wizarding world and share your thoughts and insights!
Will help if you have read all the Harry Potter books - this discussion will be based only on the books and not the movies.
The event is curated by Lahe Lahe Caravan and is being hosted at Atta Galatta.

name: விசாரணை (The Trial)  - Book Discussion in Tamil
description:
What does it mean to live under a system that offers no explanation? What does it mean to find meaning when everything has been stripped away?
The 19th Sollazhi Reader's Circle brings together two essential texts that — across different centuries and very different registers — ask the same unsettling question: what sustains a human being when reason, justice, and certainty fail?
Franz Kafka's The Trial follows Josef K., who wakes one morning to find himself arrested — without charge, without cause, without any path to understanding. A century on, it remains one of the most precise maps of bureaucratic dread and institutional absurdity ever written.
Viktor Frankl's Man's Search for Meaning is an account of survival written from inside the Nazi concentration camps — and a philosophical argument for the indestructibility of the human will. Where Kafka names the wound, Frankl searches for what heals it.
Read together, these two books open a conversation about the limits of systems, the resilience of the self, and what it means to keep searching.
7 June 2026, Sunday | 11:00 AM – 1:00 PM

Atta Galatta, Indira Nagar, Bengaluru
Organised by Sollazhi and Vishnupuram Ilakkiya Vattam, Bengaluru
Contact: 9535150062 | 9845086767 | 9986043446 | 9900324348

name: Lost in the Hyperbolic World - A Science Storytelling Session
description:
What if the world around you isn’t as straight as it feels?
This isn’t a science talk. It’s a story, one that slowly messes with how you see the world around you.
No mathematics. No equations. No prior knowledge needed.
Just a story that takes you into a hyperbolic world, where space doesn’t behave the way you expect, and leaves you wondering what was real and what wasn’t.
How would that look? How different would it be?
You may leave with a sense of what that world is like, and with more questions than answers.
Confusion to curiosity is the journey.
Age: 18+

name: The Pranic Rescue - Rescuing Our Families and Rebuilding Foundations of Love, Care & Support
description:
Pranic Healing is a revolutionary no-touch, no-drug tested energy-based treatment that uses Prana, Chi or Energy to improve a wide range of physical, psychological, relationships and financial issues. 
Learn About The Power Of Pranic Healing for Healing Our Families & Relationships.Get to know how simply and properly you can,

Eliminate everyday stress, repeated conflicts, pressure, tension, strain, emotional hurt, distance and negativity.
Bring love, understanding, harmony and togetherness into daily life.
Achieve a balanced, loving, peaceful, super-supportive home environment for all.
Questions & Answers.

name: Qu for Quiz - Quiz
description: Its Friday and its QuforQuiz. So say tata to office and be at Atta Galatta.

name: Love Algorithms - Author Interaction Event
description:
There are stories that remain on paper, and there are stories that travel beyond it; into voices, silences, frames, music, and memory. This evening is a celebration of that journey.
Bringing together literature and cinema under one roof, the event invites readers, artists, filmmakers, and storytellers into a space where conversations flow beyond mediums and stories find newer ways to breathe. At a time when the boundaries between page and screen continue to blur, this gathering stands as a quiet celebration of imagination itself; of stories becoming books, books becoming cinema, cinema becoming literature, and art constantly transforming into newer forms of emotion.
The evening will feature Aravind Sampath, author of Love Algorithms, a unique love story shaped through the presence of a chatbot that becomes a silent bridge between human emotions and connection.

name: A Song For Eresha - Author Interaction Event And Screening
description:
A. K. Srikanth, an award-winning filmmaker whose works have travelled across various international film festivals, carrying with them a deeply artistic and human voice. His acclaimed film A Song for Eresha now finds a second life in literary form through its book adaptation published by Cogitabund LLP, a sister concern of Booksthakam Pvt Ltd.
As part of the evening, audiences will also experience a screening of A Song for Eresha, creating a rare space where viewers can witness a story move between cinema and literature within the same moment. Accompanied by book signings, interactions, and shared conversations, the event hopes to become more than a gathering. It hopes to feel like an atmosphere. An atmosphere where artists meet audiences, where stories leave the confines of format, and where people come together simply because somewhere, in one way or another, art has touched their lives.

name: Tinsy Doo Tells A Tale - Storytelling & Book Signing
description:
Join author Aarushi Jain for a delightful storytelling session featuring a story from her children’s book - Tiny Tales with Tinsy Doo.
An engaging experience filled with imagination, creativity, and fun for children and parents, followed by an exclusive book signing session.
Age: 4-8 yrs
